blocks.text: Root by Sudo is a **B2C fintech app** serving underbanked Nigerians with savings, transfers, and bill payments. When I joined as Head of Design, the product had strong retention among existing users but was haemorrhaging new signups at onboarding — a problem the engineering team had been patching with dark patterns rather than fixing with good design.

The brief was deceptively simple: _reduce drop-off_. The underlying challenge was more nuanced — we needed to build trust with users who had been burned by informal savings schemes, while meeting CBN compliance requirements that made onboarding legally complex.
I ran moderated usability sessions with 12 participants across Lagos and Abuja — mix of banked and unbanked users. Three patterns emerged immediately.

items: Trust signals were absent. Users didn't know if Root was a regulated bank or an informal app — and the UI gave no indication either way.
The BVN verification step created anxiety. Users didn't understand why a 'simple savings app' needed their bank number.
Progress was invisible. The 7-step onboarding had no progress indicator, so users quit assuming there was no end.

blocks.text: I thought it was going to keep asking me for things forever. I just closed it.
The team had been treating onboarding as a compliance hurdle to get through. I reframed it as the product's first promise to the user — the moment where Root either earns trust or loses it forever.

blocks.alt: Before and after comparison of Root onboarding flow
blocks.caption: Before (left): 7 steps with no progress indication. After (right): 4 steps with trust signals at each gate.

blocks.text: The redesigned onboarding launched in Q3 2023. Within 8 weeks, onboarding completion rate moved from **41% to 66%** — a 25-point improvement that directly translated into a 3× increase in transaction volume as the cohort activated.

This project reinforced something I now treat as a design principle: _compliance requirements and good user experience are not in opposition_. The constraint is always also an opportunity to explain, reassure, and build trust.
